title: Parsnip Soup with Orange and Ginger
description: A smooth parsnip soup with orange and ginger.
ingredients: Grated rind and juice of one orange
method: Peel and dice the parsnips and onion, and carrot if using.
Melt the butter in a large pan or pressure cooker base, then stir in the parsnips, onion, flour, ginger and orange rind.
Add the stock, mix well and bring to the boil.
Cook for about 20 minutes, or until the vegetables are soft.
Purée the soup, then pass through a sieve if you want a smoother texture.
Return to the pan, stir in the orange juice and reheat gently without boiling.
Add the cream if you like and season lightly.
notes: The full quantity of stock makes a fairly thin soup.
The cream is optional.
A carrot can be added for colour.

title: Cream of Mushroom Soup
description: A rich mushroom soup finished with sherry and cream.
ingredients: For the sauce: 1/4 tsp each nutmeg and white pepper
For the sauce: a good pinch of herb salt
For the sauce: 1 glass dry sherry
method: Melt the butter and add the onion. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes.
Add the mushrooms and cover again for 5 minutes.
Add the water, stock cube, parsley and garlic, then set the mixture aside.
Using the same pan, add the butter, flour, milk, salt, nutmeg, pepper and herb salt.
Bring slowly to the boil, whisking all the time, then bubble for 5 minutes.
Stir in the mushroom mixture.
Cool in the fridge for 2 hours or overnight.
Bring back to a simmer, add extra seasoning if needed, then stir in the sherry and cream before serving.
notes: Freezes well.

title: Spanish Chargrilled Peppers
description: Chargrilled peppers with anchovies, olives and olive oil.
ingredients: 1/2 x 100g jar anchovy fillets in oil, drained and halved lengthways
method: Cut the peppers lengthways into quarters and remove the seeds.
Place the peppers skin-side up under a hot grill until the skins have charred and blackened.
Transfer immediately to a saucepan and cover with a lid.
When cool enough to handle, remove the skins.
Arrange the peppers in a shallow dish with the anchovies and olives between them.
Season with black pepper, scatter over the marjoram and pour over the olive oil.
Serve at room temperature.
notes: Good as a first course or as an accompaniment.

title: Sweet Pepper Pots
description: Soft peppers and tomatoes baked with cream, capers and Parmesan.
ingredients: 1 large red pepper, seeded and chopped
method: Melt the butter and add the pepper, onion and garlic. Cook gently, stirring often, until the peppers are soft.
Pour boiling water over the tomatoes, peel them, remove the seeds and chop the flesh.
Add the tomatoes to the pan and cook slowly for 15 minutes, until thick and soft.
Stir in the sugar, salt and a pinch of cayenne.
Spoon into small ovenproof dishes and leave to cool.
Rinse and chop the capers, then mix with the cream.
Spoon the cream mixture over the pepper base and sprinkle with Parmesan.
Bake in a very hot oven or grill until lightly browned.
notes: Fromage frais or crème fraîche can be used instead of some or all of the cream.

title: Salmon or Tuna Fish Cakes
description: Simple fish cakes made with mashed potato and parsley.
ingredients: 1/2lb / 200g canned salmon or tuna, or freshly cooked fish
method: Flake the fish and remove any skin and bones.
Combine the fish with the mashed potato, egg, onion, parsley and seasoning.
If the mixture is too soft, add 1 tbsp medium matzo meal.
If using freshly mashed potatoes, chill the mixture before shaping.
Form into 6–8 patties.
Dip in flour, then fry gently in shallow oil with a little butter or margarine until crisp and brown on both sides.
notes: If making salmon cakes, a little grated lemon rind can be added.

title: Shepherd’s Pie
description: Minced beef, vegetables, tomatoes and mashed potato baked with cheese.
ingredients: 15oz tin plum tomatoes or chopped plum tomatoes
method: Slice the onion and fry gently in margarine or oil with a little salt until soft.
Add the minced beef and fry until cooked.
Cook the mixed vegetables in a little boiling water until done, then drain.
Peel and boil the potatoes until soft, then mash with milk and margarine.
Season the meat with Worcestershire sauce and pepper, then mix in the vegetables.
Transfer the meat mixture to a dish and arrange the tomatoes on top.
Cover with the mashed potato and scatter with grated cheese.
Bake in a moderate oven until the top is golden.
notes: Crushed crisps can be added on top of the cheese for crunch.
This freezes well.

title: Moroccan Chicken and Dried Fruit Tagine
description: Chicken baked with spices, prunes, apricots and toasted almonds.
ingredients: 15 fl oz chicken stock, or half stock and half white wine
method: If the fruit is not pre-soaked, cover with boiling water for 30 minutes, then drain.
Preheat the oven to 300°F / 150°C / Gas 2 and toast the almonds as it heats.
Remove the skin and visible fat from the chicken.
Put the cake meal, salt and pepper into a bag and shake the chicken pieces in it to coat lightly.
Brown the chicken in hot oil, then place in a lidded casserole and surround with the dried fruit.
In the same oil, gently sauté the chopped onion and garlic until golden.
Add the spices, stock and honey, bring to the boil and simmer for 5 minutes.
Stir in the potato flour mixed with lemon juice or water.
Pour over the chicken, cover and bake for about 1 hour, or until tender.
Garnish with the toasted almonds before serving.
notes: The recipe notes that lamb can also be used.

title: Lemon Pasta
description: Linguine with lemon, Parmesan, basil and rocket.
ingredients: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper
method: Cook the pasta in plenty of boiling salted water for about 10 minutes, then drain well and return to the pan.
Beat the lemon juice and zest with the olive oil, then stir in the Parmesan until thick and creamy.
Season to taste.
Add the lemon sauce to the pasta and shake the pan so each strand is coated.
Finish by stirring in the chopped basil and rocket.

title: Roasted garlic potato mash
description: Creamy mashed potatoes with roasted garlic and crème fraîche.
ingredients: 2 heads of garlic, top third cut off
2 lb / 900g red-skinned potatoes, peeled and cut into chunks
method: Heat the oven to 200°C / 400°F / Gas 6.
Place the garlic on foil, drizzle with olive oil, wrap loosely and bake for 45–50 minutes until golden and soft.
Boil the potatoes in a large pot with the salt until soft, then drain and return to the pot.
Mash with the butter until smooth.
Add the crème fraîche and black pepper.
Squeeze the roasted garlic cloves out of their skins and mix gently into the potatoes.
Taste and adjust seasoning.
notes: Can be prepared earlier in the day and reheated gently.

title: Coldslaw
description: A cabbage and carrot slaw with mayonnaise and soured cream.
ingredients: Salt and freshly ground black pepper
method: Shred the cabbage and dry it well.
Mix together the vinegar, sugar, salt, pepper, mayonnaise and soured cream.
Add the cabbage, carrots and chives.
Toss lightly and adjust seasoning.
notes: The original notes mention optional steps to crisp the cabbage and marinate it first, but they can be skipped.

title: Chocolate Mousse I
description: A classic chocolate mousse with eggs and optional sherry or brandy.
method: Separate the eggs.
Beat the egg whites until stiff.
Melt the chocolate over hot water or in the microwave.
Add the egg yolks to the chocolate and beat well.
Add the sherry or brandy if using.
Fold in 1 tablespoon of the beaten egg whites, then fold in the rest gently.
Refrigerate until needed.

title: Lemon Cake
description: A simple lemon cake finished with a lemon icing syrup.
ingredients: For the syrup: 3 tbsp lemon juice
For the syrup: 3 tbsp icing sugar
method: Put all the cake ingredients into a bowl and beat until mixed.
Bake in a greased and floured 7½-inch round tin or loaf tin for about 1 hour at 350°F / 160°C / Gas 4.
When cold, prick the cake all over.
Warm the lemon juice and icing sugar together to make a syrup.
Pour the syrup over the cake.

title: Chicken soup
description: A simple traditional chicken soup with leeks, carrots, celery and parsley.
ingredients: Chicken stock made from carcass or boiling fowl
method: Slice the leeks, carrots and celery.
Chop the onion and parsley.
Add the vegetables and herbs to the stock.
Bring to the boil, then simmer until the vegetables are tender.
Season with salt and pepper.

title: Minted Pea Soup
description: A quick pea soup with spring onions and lettuce, blended until smooth.
method: Sauté the chopped spring onions.
Add the lettuce leaves and sauté briefly.
Add the peas, stock cubes and water.
Cook until the peas are tender.
Blend in a food processor or liquidiser until smooth.
Serve hot with a little cream on top if desired.

title: Piquant Tomato Soup
description: A rich tomato soup with onion, carrot, celery, oregano and lemon rind.
ingredients: 1 tbsp rendered chicken fat or 1 oz / 25g margarine
1 can whole peeled plum tomatoes with juice
2 pints / 1 litre meat, bone, chicken or vegetable stock
Optional: 1 tbsp cornflour mixed with 2 tbsp cold water
Optional: a handful of fine vermicelli
method: Melt the fat gently in a heavy soup pan.
Add the sliced vegetables, cover, and cook gently for about 10 minutes until softened without browning.
Add the tomatoes and all the remaining main ingredients.
Cover and simmer for 45 minutes until the vegetables are tender.
Remove the bay leaf.
Push through a sieve or blend until smooth.
Return the soup to the pan and taste.
Add a little more tomato purée if needed.
If you want a thicker soup, add the cornflour mixture and simmer for 5 minutes.
If using vermicelli, cook it either in the soup or separately and add before serving.
notes: Cornflour and vermicelli are optional.
source: Evelyn Rose, Complete International Jewish Cookbook

title: Chestnut Soup
description: A smooth chestnut soup with onion, celery and carrot, finished with cream if liked.
method: Cut a slit on the flat side of each chestnut.
Cover with boiling water, reboil and simmer for 10 minutes.
Drain and peel away the outer and inner skins.
Melt the butter in a large saucepan.
Peel and finely chop the onion, add it to the pan, cover and fry gently for about 5 minutes until tender but not coloured.
Add the chestnuts, celery and carrot.
Add the stock and bring to the boil.
Cover and simmer for 45 minutes to 1 hour until the vegetables are tender.
Liquidise, blend or sieve to a purée.
Return to the pan and season with salt and plenty of pepper.
Stir in the cream and reheat until hot but not boiling.
Serve with parsley if using.
notes: Cream is optional and can also be added in decorative swirls when serving.

title: Spring Greens and Roast Garlic Soup
description: A green soup with roast garlic, potatoes, spring greens and cream.
method: Roast the garlic for about 40-50 minutes in a preheated oven at 190°C / 375°F / Gas 5 until completely tender, adding a little water from time to time if needed.
Melt the butter in a large heavy-based saucepan.
Add the onion, spring onion and potato.
Stir so that the vegetables are coated in the butter, then add a splash of water.
Cover and let the vegetables sweat over a low heat for about 15 minutes, checking occasionally and adding a little more water if needed.
Remove the tough ribs from the spring greens and slice the leafy parts.
Add them to the pan with a splash of water and seasoning.
Cover and cook for about 4 minutes, turning the leaves occasionally.
Squeeze the soft garlic pulp from the cooked cloves into a bowl.
Add the garlic purée and the stock to the vegetables and bring to the boil.
Turn off the heat and leave to cool.
Purée the soup with the cream.
Check the seasoning and serve hot.
notes: A little of the stock can be held back until puréeing to adjust the final consistency.
Serve with a drizzle of cream on top if liked.

title: Thai Pumpkin Soup
description: A smooth pumpkin or butternut squash soup with ginger, coconut milk and basil cream.
ingredients: 1kg pumpkin or butternut squash, peeled, deseeded and cut into 1cm cubes
2.5cm piece fresh root ginger, peeled and finely chopped
method: Preheat the oven to 180°C / Gas 4.
Place the pumpkin or squash on a non-stick baking tray, season, and roast for 30 minutes or until tender.
Meanwhile heat the oil in a medium pan and add the onion and ginger.
Cover and sauté for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the curry paste and cook for 1-2 minutes.
Add the roasted pumpkin, stock and coconut milk.
Cover, bring to the boil, then simmer for 5 minutes.
Remove from the heat and cool slightly.
Purée in a food processor or blender in batches until smooth.
Return to the pan, season to taste and reheat gently.
Add a little more stock or boiling water if you want a thinner consistency.
Heat the cream and chopped basil gently in a small pan.
Serve the soup with the basil cream swirled over the top.
notes: The source notes that cumin powder can be used instead of Thai curry paste.

title: Grilled Summer Vegetable Timbales
description: A layered starter of aubergine, tomato, mozzarella and avocado with basil dressing.
ingredients: 1 large avocado, stoned, peeled and sliced
method: Heat the oven to 200°C / 400°F / Gas 6.
Cut the aubergine lengthways into thin slices about 6mm thick.
Quarter and de-seed the tomatoes.
Place the aubergine and tomatoes on a baking sheet, drizzle with 1 tbsp of oil and season.
Roast for about 20-30 minutes until golden, turning once.
Use the aubergine slices to line four small ramekins, leaving the ends hanging over the edge.
Whisk together the balsamic vinegar, basil, garlic and remaining olive oil with seasoning.
Layer the mozzarella, avocado and tomatoes in the ramekins, pressing down and drizzling with the dressing as you go.
Fold the aubergine ends over the top and press down well.
Cover and chill until ready to serve.
Turn out onto serving dishes and garnish with salad leaves if using.
notes: Based on the classic combination of tomato, mozzarella and avocado.

title: Herring Salad
description: A chopped salad of pickled herrings, beetroot, potato, apple and cucumber bound with salad cream.
ingredients: 1 or 2 cooking apples or sharp eating apples
2 or 3 tbsp salad cream, to taste
method: Drain the pickled herrings and onions, and drain the pickled cucumbers.
Chop the herrings, beetroot, potatoes, apples, cucumbers and egg finely.
Add chopped nuts and raisins.
Mix with salad cream to taste.
Adjust the balance of ingredients to your liking before serving.
notes: Can also include salt, pepper, onions, vinegar or wine if liked.
The source notes that there should be at least seven ingredients excluding the salad cream.

title: Herrings with Apple in Sour Cream
description: Rollmops with apple and onion mixed into sour cream.
ingredients: 1 eating apple with skin, quartered and cored
method: Dry the herrings and onions on kitchen paper.
Cut the herrings, onions and apple into pieces.
Mix with the sour cream.
Add a little sugar to taste.

title: Egg and Mushroom Ramekins
description: Baked eggs with mushrooms, cream and Parmesan in individual ramekins.
method: Put a knob of butter in each ramekin.
Add some sliced mushrooms to each ramekin.
Crack an egg on top.
Cover with cream.
Sprinkle generously with Parmesan.
Season with salt and pepper.
Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F / 160°C for 15-20 minutes.

title: Chopped Liver
description: A traditional chopped liver mixture with onions and hard-boiled eggs.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
method: Wash and drain the livers and remove the outer skin and fat.
Fry the onions in fat until soft, then add the chicken livers.
Fry gently until cooked, then allow to cool.
Put the livers and eggs through a mincer.
Mix with enough fat or oil to bind.
Refrigerate before serving.
notes: Decorate with sieved hard-boiled egg and serve with challah.

title: Chopped Liver en Croute
description: Chopped liver wrapped in puff pastry and baked.
method: Use about 1½ lb liver mixture to 1 lb puff pastry.
Roll out the puff pastry into a rectangle.
Shape the chopped liver into a sausage and place it on the pastry.
Roll the pastry around the liver and seal.
Bake at 400°F / 170°C until cooked.
notes: Serve hot with lettuce leaves, tomato and hot barbecue sauce.

title: Egg and Onion
description: A simple chopped egg mixture with onion and seasoning.
ingredients: Additional fat and/or mayonnaise for binding
method: Chop the eggs.
Prepare the onion, softened first if preferred.
Add the onion and seasoning to the eggs.
Mix with fat or mayonnaise to bind.
notes: The onion is nicer if softened first in chicken fat or sunflower margarine.

title: Egg and Mushroom en Cocotte
description: Eggs baked over mushrooms with cream and Parmesan.
method: Put a small knob of butter or margarine in each ramekin.
Place sliced mushrooms in the bottom of each.
Add one egg on top.
Season with salt and pepper.
Add a layer of double cream.
Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
Cook in a preheated oven at 350°F / 160°C for 15-20 minutes.

title: Egg and Chicken Livers en Cocotte
description: Baked eggs topped with a chicken liver sauce.
method: Put a small knob of butter or margarine in each ramekin.
Place one egg in each ramekin.
Cook for 15-20 minutes at 350°F.
Cut the chicken livers into pieces and fry them.
Thicken with cornflour mixed with water.
Season with salt and pepper and add some tomato ketchup.
Pour the liver mixture over the cooked eggs.
Garnish with chopped parsley.

title: Barbecued Ribs I
description: Lamb ribs marinated in a sweet and savoury sauce, then baked.
ingredients: 2 lbs lamb ribs, cut into strips
method: Mix all ingredients except the ribs.
Marinate the ribs in the mixture for about an hour.
Place the ribs on a rack.
Bake at 350°F / 160°C for 1¼ hours, basting frequently.
If needed, put under the grill briefly at the end to brown.

title: Barbecued Ribs II
description: A quick barbecue sauce for ribs.
method: Warm all the ingredients together until combined.

title: Fish Cocktail
description: Flaked fish with orange, lettuce and a pink mayonnaise sauce.
ingredients: 1 orange, peeled, segmented and cut into pieces
For the sauce: 1-1½ tbsp tomato sauce
For the sauce: a few drops Worcestershire sauce
method: Boil the fish with the onion, parsley and lemon juice.
Mix the sauce ingredients together.
Shred lettuce into glasses or serving dishes.
Flake the fish and mix with the orange pieces.
Place the fish mixture on top of the lettuce.
Pour over the sauce and sprinkle with paprika.

title: Whitebait
description: Whitebait lightly coated and quickly deep fried.
method: Dip the whitebait in milk.
Shake in a cloth with flour until lightly coated.
Deep fry for 1-2 minutes.

title: Piperonata
description: Soft peppers, tomatoes and onion cooked gently with garlic and a little sugar.
cookTime: About 10 minutes or longer to soften fully
ingredients: 6 large red, green and yellow peppers, seeded, cored and cut into thin strips
8 large tomatoes, skinned and coarsely chopped
1 large clove garlic, peeled and crushed
method: Sweat the onion and peppers with the butter and olive oil in a heavy-based pan.
Cover and cook until the vegetables are soft but not brown.
Add the tomatoes, garlic, seasoning and sugar.
Cover and continue cooking over a very low heat, stirring occasionally, until the mixture is soft and the juices have largely evaporated.
notes: Serve hot or cold, but not chilled.

title: Aubergine Dip I
description: A simple blended aubergine dip with onion and seasoning.
method: Prick the aubergine and cook it whole in the microwave.
Blend with the remaining ingredients.
Serve cold.

title: Aubergine Dip II
description: A blended aubergine dip with spring onion and salad cream.
method: Wash and slice the aubergine, removing the skin if preferred.
Cook in the microwave until soft and allow to cool.
Blend with the spring onion and enough salad cream to taste.
Season if needed and serve cold.

title: Liptauer Cheese
description: A quick cheese spread with paprika and flavoured cream cheeses.
method: Mix all the ingredients together until blended.
notes: Pickled cucumbers or other additions can be included.

title: Cold Salmon Mousse
description: A smooth salmon mousse made with béchamel, cream and a little sherry.
ingredients: For the béchamel: 3 oz butter or margarine
For finishing: 2 tbsp lightly whipped double cream
method: Boil the water with lemon juice, salt and peppercorns.
Place the salmon in the pan, cover and cook very gently for about 15 minutes.
Allow to cool, then drain and remove all skin and bones.
Heat the milk with the bay leaf, mace, peppercorns and onion, then strain and cool.
Melt 1 oz butter, blend in the flour and then the flavoured milk.
Add salt, stir over gentle heat until boiling, and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Turn the sauce into a dish and allow it to cool.
Cream the remaining butter and mash the salmon.
Add the cold béchamel sauce and butter mixture to the salmon and taste for seasoning.
Fold in the cream and sherry.
Turn into a serving dish, smooth the top and chill until set.
Decorate with sliced cucumber.
notes: Do not boil the salmon hard.

title: Salmon in Watercress Sauce
description: Salmon served with a quick cream sauce made from cooking juices and watercress.
method: Cook the salmon steaks either in the microwave or by pan-frying in butter or oil, finishing in the microwave if needed.
Using the cooking juices, add chopped watercress, salt, pepper, lemon juice and cream.
Boil until the sauce thickens.
Pour over the salmon and serve.

title: White Fish in Coconut Cream Sauce
description: White fish cooked gently with onion, garlic, coconut milk and aromatic spices.
method: Fry the chopped onion and garlic.
Add the coconut milk mixture, curry leaves, cardamom pods and dried lime.
Cook gently for a while to develop the flavour.
Add the diced fish and cook until done.
Serve on rice.

title: Fish Balls
description: Minced fish balls that can be fried or cooked in fish stock.
ingredients: 1/2 large onion, minced or finely chopped
method: Place the minced fish in a bowl.
Add the egg, salt, pepper, sugar and ground almonds if using.
Mix everything together.
Add enough matzo meal to make a mixture that can just be formed into balls.
If it is too stiff, add a little water. If too wet, add more matzo meal.
Leave for about half an hour before shaping.
Fry in deep fat for about 6 minutes until richly browned.
notes: The balls can also be cooked in fish stock instead of frying.

title: Dawn’s Phyllo Topped Salmon Pie
description: A salmon pie with peas, optional leeks, a creamy sauce and a crisp phyllo topping.
ingredients: Phyllo pastry, sheets cut in half
method: Heat the oven to 350-375°F / Gas 6 / 160°C fan oven.
Place the salmon in the base of a shallow dish.
Add the peas and leeks if using, and mix lightly.
Mix the Hollandaise sauce with some crème fraîche if using.
Pour the sauce over the salmon mixture.
Sprinkle with grated cheese.
Take the half sheets of phyllo pastry, scrunch each one loosely in the middle, and place them over the top.
Continue until the dish is covered.
Dot with butter or brush with melted butter.
Bake for 20-30 minutes until golden brown.
notes: Using crème fraîche makes the sauce lighter than using only Hollandaise.

title: Fillets of Trout with Sour-Cream Sauce and Dill Cucumber
description: Trout fillets served with a warm sour-cream sauce and cucumber.
ingredients: A good squeeze of lemon juice
method: Dip the trout fillets in seasoned flour to coat lightly on both sides.
Shake off the excess flour.
Melt the butter or margarine in a frying pan until foaming.
Cook the trout gently on both sides, turning once the first side is firm and lightly golden.
Remove the fish and keep warm.
Add the sour cream to the pan and turn the heat down.
Heat the cream through gently without boiling.
Add lemon juice, salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the trout with the sour-cream sauce spooned over.
notes: The source suggests serving with boiled waxy potatoes and Cucumber Salad II.

title: Pickled Herrings
description: Herrings layered with a tangy cream, mustard and cucumber mixture and left to mature.
method: Prepare the herrings by removing the heads and tails, splitting, cleaning, boning and skinning them.
Alternatively, use herrings prepared by the fishmonger and just remove any remaining bones.
Mix all the other ingredients together.
Layer the mixture with the herrings in a dish.
Seal and keep in a cool place for at least 6 days, turning daily.
notes: They will keep for at least another week in the refrigerator.

title: Roast White Fish with Salsa Verde
description: Simple roast fish served with salsa verde and lemon.
method: Begin by making the salsa verde.
Heat the oven to 200°C / Gas 6.
Line a small baking dish with foil and add a splash of olive oil.
Arrange the fish fillets in the dish and splash with a little more oil.
Squeeze over the lemon juice and season with salt and pepper.
Roast for 6-10 minutes depending on thickness, until just cooked through.
Serve with the juices, a generous spoonful of salsa verde and lemon wedges.

title: Left-over Turkey Strudel
description: A puff pastry strudel filled with leftover turkey, stuffing, mushrooms and vegetables.
ingredients: Left-over turkey, sliced and broken into pieces
method: Sauté the onion in a little margarine for about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped mushrooms and continue sautéing for another 5 minutes, then allow to cool.
Roll out the pastry into a thin large square or oblong and place on a baking tray.
Sprinkle breadcrumbs down the centre of the pastry.
Spread half the mushroom mixture on top, then add the stuffing.
Place the turkey on top and cover with the remaining mushroom mixture and any leftover vegetables.
Moisten the pastry edges with water, fold over to make a parcel and seal the ends.
Brush the top with milk or water and sprinkle with coarse salt and black pepper.
Bake at 400°F until golden brown, about 1 hour.
notes: Asparagus tips are suggested among the leftover vegetables.

title: Beef Cranberry Casserole
description: A slow-cooked beef casserole with onions, port, cinnamon, basil and cranberries.
method: Heat the oil in a frying pan and brown the meat for 5-10 minutes.
Transfer the meat to a large casserole dish.
Fry the onions in the pan juices until soft and add them to the casserole.
Combine the flour, salt, pepper, cinnamon and basil.
Blend this into the remaining pan juices, adding a little stock if needed.
Add the port and the rest of the stock, stirring well, and bring to the boil.
Adjust the seasoning and pour over the meat.
Stir in the cranberries, cover and cook in the oven for 2½-3 hours.
Garnish with parsley if liked.
notes: Suggested accompaniments in the source are jacket potatoes, broccoli and sautéed courgettes.

title: Beef with Prunes
description: Stewing beef braised slowly with onion, garlic, red wine and prunes.
method: Brown cubed beef, lightly coated in flour, in hot fat.
Remove the meat and keep warm.
Add the sliced onion and chopped garlic to the pan and soften.
Add the wine and water, stir well and bring to the boil.
Pour over the meat.
Add the prunes and season with salt and pepper.
Cook in the oven at a low heat for about 3 hours, or until the meat and prunes are tender.
notes: If no wine is available, beef stock can be used instead.

title: Tangy Venison and Apricot Casserole
description: Venison casserole with dried apricots, chutney, Worcestershire sauce and pistachios.
method: Coat the venison in seasoned flour.
Heat the oil in a pan and cook the venison for about 5 minutes until browned.
Add the onion and cook for a further 2 minutes.
Transfer to an ovenproof casserole dish.
Add the apricots, chutney, Worcestershire sauce, sugar, stock, salt and pepper.
Cover and cook in a preheated oven at 160°C / 325°F / Gas 3 for about 1½ hours, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle with chopped pistachios before serving.

title: Frances Perry’s Chicken Dish
description: Chicken baked on a bed of onion, raisins and apricots with marmalade and white wine.
method: Place the chopped onion, raisins and halved apricots in the base of a cooking tin.
Place the chicken on top.
Spread marmalade over the chicken.
Pour white wine over the top.
Season with salt and pepper.
Cook until the chicken is done.

title: Margot Haas’ Chicken Dish
description: Chicken baked with sliced onion, mango chutney, mustard and pine nuts.
method: Arrange the chicken pieces in an oven dish, skin side up.
Season well with salt, pepper and chicken seasoning.
Cover with the sliced onion and then the mango chutney.
Rinse each chutney jar with the mustard, tomato purée and warm water.
Pour the liquid over the chicken.
Cover with foil and cook at 350-375°F / Gas 6 / 160°C fan oven for 40 minutes.
Remove the foil, add the pine nuts and return to the oven for a further 30 minutes to brown the top.

title: Whole Curried Chicken with Saffron Rice
description: A whole chicken stuffed with curried rice, baked in curry sauce and served with saffron rice and chappati.
ingredients: For the stuffing: 4oz long grain rice
For the stuffing: 1 oz butter or margarine
For the stuffing: 1 small onion, finely chopped
For the stuffing: 1 dessertspoon curry powder
For the curry sauce: 1½ oz butter or margarine
For the curry sauce: 1 small onion, finely chopped
For the curry sauce: 3 tbsp curry powder
For the curry sauce: 1 rounded tbsp plain flour
For the curry sauce: 1 pint chicken stock
For the curry sauce: 1 tbsp mango chutney
For the curry sauce: 1 rounded tbsp soft brown sugar
For the curry sauce: juice of 1/2 lemon
For the curry sauce: 1/2 tsp salt
For the saffron rice: 8oz long grain rice
For the saffron rice: a pinch of saffron
For the saffron rice: a pinch of granulated sugar
For the saffron rice: 2 tbsp hot water
For the chappati: 1 level tsp salt
For the chappati: water to mix
method: Wipe the chicken and remove any giblets.
Prepare the stuffing by boiling the rice briefly in salted water, then draining it.
Melt the butter or margarine in a small pan, add the onion and fry gently until soft.
Stir in the curry powder, then the cooked rice and salt.
Spoon the stuffing into the chicken cavity, close it and place the chicken in a large casserole dish.
For the curry sauce, melt the butter or margarine in a saucepan, add the onion and cook gently until soft.
Stir in the curry powder and cook for about 5 minutes.
Stir in the flour, then gradually add the chicken stock.
Bring to the boil and add the mango chutney, sugar, lemon juice and salt.
Cover and simmer gently for about 30 minutes.
Strain the sauce over the chicken.
Cover the casserole and cook in a slow oven for 3-4 hours, basting occasionally.
About 15 minutes before serving, prepare the saffron rice by cooking the rice in boiling salted water until tender, then draining it.
Grind the saffron with the sugar, mix with the hot water and leave to infuse for 10 minutes.
Strain the saffron infusion into the cooked rice and mix until the grains are coloured yellow.
For the chappati, place the flour and salt in a mixing bowl.
Gradually add enough water to make a stiff dough.
Knead until smooth and elastic, then leave to rest for about 1 hour if possible.
Divide the dough into pieces and roll each one thinly into a circle.
Cook on an ungreased hot heavy frying pan until lightly browned on both sides.
Then place briefly under a hot grill so the chappati puffs up.
Serve the chicken with the curry sauce, saffron rice and chappati.
notes: The source notes that the longer the chicken cooks, the better the flavour.
Chappati is included here as part of the full dish.

title: Chicken Bearnaise
description: A whole chicken browned in butter and oil, baked with garlic and stock.
method: Heat the oven to 400°F / 200°C / Gas 6.
Rub the chicken with the lemon and season with salt and pepper.
Heat the butter and oil in a large casserole and brown the chicken all over.
Boil the garlic cloves for 1 minute, cool them and slip off the skins.
Scatter the garlic around the chicken with the stock.
Cover and bake for 45 minutes.
Remove the lid, baste the chicken, then return uncovered to the oven for 15-30 minutes until browned and cooked through.
Rest the chicken briefly before carving.
Whisk the juices to make a gravy and serve with the garlic.

title: Balsamic Chicken with Porcini Mushrooms and Sun-dried Cherries
description: Chicken thighs simmered with porcini, pancetta, balsamic vinegar, red wine and dried cherries.
ingredients: 4 oz / 110g diced pancetta or chopped smoked bacon
method: Pour the boiling water over the porcini mushrooms and leave for 30 minutes.
Strain, reserving the liquid, and chop the mushrooms.
Dredge the chicken in seasoned flour.
Fry the pancetta until crisp, then remove it.
Brown the chicken in the bacon fat and remove it.
Sauté the onion and garlic until soft.
Stir in the porcini mushrooms, cherries, balsamic vinegar, red wine, stock and some of the reserved porcini liquid.
Bring to the boil and simmer for 10 minutes.
Return the chicken to the pan and simmer for about 30 minutes.
Sprinkle with chopped parsley before serving.
notes: The source notes that the sauce can be thickened with a little flour mixed with water.

title: Anthony’s Curry Recipe
description: A flexible curry using chicken or vegetables with ginger, garlic, spices and coconut.
ingredients: Fresh ginger, cut into thin strips
Cumin, coriander powder and/or garam masala
Coconut milk or cream and desiccated coconut
method: Heat a fair amount of oil in a pan.
Fry the spices, ginger and garlic slowly to develop the flavour.
Add the meat or chicken first, then add vegetables in order of cooking time.
When the curry is fried and partly cooked, add the coconut-based sauce and fresh coriander.
Continue cooking until everything is ready.
Serve with rice.
notes: Suggested Thai-style combinations include chicken with green beans or mixed vegetables with pumpkin or cucumber.
Suggested Indian-style variation includes chicken and spinach.

title: Chicken Livers with Rice
description: Chicken livers cooked with onion, mushrooms, tomato and sherry, served on rice.
method: Sauté the chopped onion in butter and oil until transparent.
Add the livers, washed, dried and halved, and season with salt and pepper.
Cook for about 1 minute, then remove and keep hot.
Add the mushrooms to the pan and simmer briefly, then add them to the livers.
Add the tomato purée, tomato, stock and sherry to the pan and simmer for a few minutes.
Return the livers and mushrooms to the pan and cook through.
Serve on a bed of rice.

title: Tasty Chicken Livers
description: Chicken livers with onion, mushrooms, tomato and sherry, served hot on rice.
method: Simmer the chopped onion in the butter and oil until transparent.
Add the livers, washed, dried and halved, season with salt and pepper, and cook briefly.
Remove the livers and keep hot.
Simmer the mushrooms in the same pan and add them to the livers.
Add the tomato purée, tomato, stock and sherry to the pan and simmer for a few minutes.
Return the mushrooms and livers to the pan and warm through.
Serve on a bed of rice.

title: Duck in Orange Sauce
description: Roast duck served with a citrus and brandy sauce.
ingredients: For the sauce: 1/4 pint chicken stock
method: Heat the oven to a hot setting.
Roast the duck for about 3/4 hour with a little water, pricking the skin frequently to reduce the fat.
Pour off the fat.
Rub the duck with honey and season with salt and pepper.
Slice the onion, crush the garlic and add both to the duck with a little more water.
Roast for a further 1¾ hours.
For the sauce, squeeze the oranges and lemon into a saucepan with the brandy and chicken stock.
Bring to the boil and thicken with arrowroot mixed with a little cold water.
Cut the duck into portions and pour over the sauce.

title: Wienner Schnitzel and Chicken Escalopes
description: Crumbed veal or chicken escalopes fried until golden.
ingredients: Egg, beaten with a little cold water
method: Flatten the escalopes between cling film.
Mix the dry ingredients and coat the escalopes on both sides.
Dip in the beaten egg.
Coat again in the crumb mixture.
Fry in margarine or fat until golden on both sides.
notes: For chicken escalopes, omit the oregano and dip first in seasoned flour, then egg, then matzo meal.

title: Blanquette of Veal
description: A classic veal stew with a pale creamy sauce and lemon finish.
ingredients: For the sauce: 1½ oz butter or margarine
For the sauce: 1½ oz plain flour
For the sauce: 1/4 pint single cream or creamy milk
For the sauce: a squeeze of lemon juice
method: Cut the veal into pieces and soak in plenty of cold salted water overnight.
Drain and cover with fresh cold water, add the lemon slice and salt, then bring to the boil.
Drain and refresh.
Return to the pan, barely cover with cold water, bring slowly to the boil and skim well.
Add a little salt and the bouquet garni, then simmer for about 45 minutes.
Add the quartered vegetables and continue to simmer until the meat is very tender.
Strain off the liquor and reserve it, keeping the meat and vegetables warm.
For the sauce, melt the butter, stir in the flour and cook briefly without colouring.
Blend in 1 pint of the veal stock and return to the heat.
Stir until boiling and cook for 3-4 minutes.
Add half the milk or cream and adjust seasoning.
Mix the yolks with the remaining milk, add as a liaison, and finish with lemon juice.
Remove the bouquet garni and pour the sauce over the meat and vegetables.
Warm through gently before serving.
notes: Suggested accompaniments are boiled rice or potato purée.

title: Beef Burgers
description: Simple homemade beef burgers with onion, egg and matzo meal.
method: Combine all the ingredients thoroughly.
Leave for about half an hour.
Form into patties.
Fry gently in shallow fat until browned.
notes: For 2 lb beef, double the other ingredients but use 3 eggs.

title: Meatballs in Tomato Sauce
description: Minced beef meatballs cooked in a tomato and garlic sauce.
ingredients: 2 oz breadcrumbs or medium matzo meal
method: Mix the beef with 1 grated onion, beaten egg, breadcrumbs, salt and pepper to make meatballs.
Soften the remaining chopped onion and garlic in a little oil.
Add the meatballs and brown them.
Drain off any excess fat.
Add the tomato purée, tomatoes, Italian seasoning, sugar, salt and pepper.
Bring to the boil.
Cover and simmer for 40 minutes, or bake in the oven at 350°F / 160°C / Gas 4 for 50 minutes.
notes: Add a little beef stock if the sauce reduces too much.

title: Spaghetti alla Bolognese or Stuffed Peppers I
description: A meat sauce that can be served with spaghetti or used to fill peppers.
cookTime: About 45 minutes plus pasta or pepper baking time
method: Coarsely mince the onion, carrot and celery.
Heat the oil and butter and gently fry the vegetables and garlic for 5 minutes.
Add the minced beef and cook for a further 5 minutes, stirring continuously.
Add the tomatoes, tomato purée, wine and bay leaves.
Simmer for about 3/4 hour.
Remove the bay leaves and season to taste.
Cook the pasta separately in boiling salted water, drain and serve with the sauce and Parmesan.
notes: For stuffed peppers, partly cook the peppers first, make the sauce thicker by cooking it down further, fill the peppers and bake at 350°F / 160°C / Gas 4 for 20-30 minutes.

title: Stuffed Peppers II
description: Peppers filled with minced beef, onion and walnuts in a simple tomato sauce.
method: Soften the chopped onion in a little oil.
Add the beef and brown it.
Drain off excess fat.
Cut the stem end off the peppers and remove the seeds.
Stuff the peppers with the meat mixture.
Place in a shallow ovenproof dish.
Mix the tomato purée with water, salt and pepper and pour over the peppers.
Bake covered at 350°F / 160°C / Gas 4 for 30 minutes, then uncover and cook for a further 20 minutes.

title: Val’s Fruity Chicken
description: Chicken marinated with vinegar, olives, capers, prunes and apricots, then baked with wine and sugar.
ingredients: 1 cup dried apricots, cut into pieces
method: Mix all ingredients except the sugar and white wine.
Place the chicken in an ovenproof dish and pour over the marinade.
Leave to marinate overnight, turning the chicken occasionally if possible.
Before cooking, add the sugar and white wine and mix.
Bake uncovered at 180°C for 30-40 minutes until cooked.

title: Barbequed Lamb Ribs
description: Lamb ribs marinated in a sweet and savoury glaze, then baked and browned.
ingredients: 2 lb lamb ribs cut into strips
3 tbsp duck sauce or chicken stock
method: Mix all ingredients except the ribs.
Marinate the ribs in the mixture for about an hour.
Put the ribs on a rack and bake at 350°F for 1¼ hours, basting frequently.
Finish briefly under the grill to brown if needed.

title: Slow-cooked Lamb with Puy Lentils
description: Slow-roasted lamb shoulder with onion, garlic, wine, lentils, tomatoes and mint.
method: Heat the oven to a high setting.
Season the lamb well with salt and pepper.
Scatter the onion in the base of a casserole and place the lamb on top.
Sprinkle with the garlic, oregano, lemon juice, olive oil and wine.
Season again and cover tightly.
Cook for 20 minutes, then reduce the heat and continue cooking for about 3 hours until the lamb is tender.
Cook the lentils separately in water until just tender, then drain.
Remove the lamb from the oven, mix the lentils, tomatoes and mint into the juices around the meat.
Cover again and return to the oven for a further 20 minutes.
Pull the lamb apart or carve and serve with the lentils.
notes: The source notes that the lentils can be added earlier with extra liquid, but then need checking during cooking.

title: Kleftico
description: A very simple slow-roasted lamb dish with herbs, spices and root vegetables.
method: Place all the ingredients in a roasting or casserole dish.
Add a little water.
Slow roast in a low oven until the lamb is tender and cooked through.

title: Asparagus Risotto
description: A simple risotto with asparagus, onion, parsley and Parmesan.
ingredients: 1 lb asparagus, whole or in pieces
method: Prepare the asparagus and cut into pieces if needed.
Simmer the asparagus in a little water for about 4-5 minutes, then drain and reserve the cooking water.
Melt a little butter in a large pan or wok and soften the chopped onion with a little salt.
Add the rice and stir so that it is coated in the butter.
Add some of the asparagus water and cook over a very low heat, stirring from time to time.
Keep adding more asparagus water, then plain water and stock powder as needed, until the rice is cooked.
Shortly before the rice is ready, add the asparagus and parsley.
Stir in some Parmesan, adjust seasoning and serve with extra Parmesan.

title: Spinach Risotto
description: A creamy risotto with mushrooms, spinach, pine nuts and Parmesan.
ingredients: 25 fl oz / 700ml light chicken or vegetable stock
8 oz / 225g fresh spinach, washed and shredded
method: Heat the olive oil in a large pan or wok.
Add the garlic, mushrooms and onion and fry for about 5 minutes until softened.
Add the rice and stir for 2 minutes.
Pour in the wine, bring to the boil and bubble briefly.
Add the stock a ladle at a time, stirring and allowing each addition to be absorbed before adding more.
When the rice is cooked but still slightly al dente, stir in the spinach until wilted.
Season with salt and pepper.
Stir in the pine nuts and butter.
Sprinkle with Parmesan and serve.

title: Rice
description: Plain rice cooked in stock with chopped spring onion.
method: Cook the rice in chicken stock or stock made with a cube.
Add chopped spring onion when the rice is cooked.

title: Red Cabbage
description: Braised red cabbage with pears, onion, red wine, honey and sloe gin.
ingredients: 1 small or 1/2 large red cabbage, thinly sliced
3 pears, peeled, cored and cut into cubes
method: Heat the oven to 170°C.
Place all ingredients except a small amount of the sloe gin into a casserole.
Season with salt and pepper.
Bring to the simmer, cover and braise in the oven for about 2 hours until syrupy.
Check partway through and add a little water if the cabbage seems dry.
Stir in the remaining sloe gin before serving.
notes: If sloe gin is not available, crème de cassis or extra red wine can be used.

title: Austrian Red Cabbage
description: Red cabbage cooked with onion, apple, vinegar and cloves or caraway.
ingredients: 2 large cooking apples, peeled, cored and sliced
method: Melt the fat in a large saucepan and fry the onion and garlic for about 5 minutes.
Stir in the cabbage and apples and simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the cloves or caraway, vinegar, water, sugar and seasoning.
Bring to the boil, cover and simmer until the liquid has been absorbed and the cabbage is tender.
notes: This can also be cooked in the microwave.
Freezes well.

title: Garlicky Beans
description: Fine beans tossed with butter and crushed garlic.
ingredients: Fine beans, French beans or similar
method: Prepare the beans and cook until al dente.
Add a knob of butter or margarine and the crushed garlic.
Mix and serve.

title: Courgettes with Almonds
description: Courgettes and onion cooked gently and finished with ground almonds.
method: Melt the fat in a frying pan and cook the sliced onion with a little salt until almost soft.
Slice the courgettes and add them to the onions.
Cook until al dente.
Add the ground almonds and mix so they absorb the juices.
Serve hot.

title: Potato Latkes
description: Simple grated potato pancakes fried until golden.
method: Grate the potatoes on a coarse grater.
Spoon into shallow hot oil.
Fry until golden on one side, then turn and fry the other side until golden.
notes: Optional additions include salt, pepper, flour and chopped onion.

title: Sprouts and Carrots
description: Cooked sprouts and carrots served in a simple onion sauce.
method: Prepare the sprouts and cut a small cross in the bottom.
Slice the carrots and cook them in salted water for about 5-6 minutes.
Add the sprouts and continue cooking until both vegetables are tender.
Drain, reserving the cooking liquid.
In another pan, melt butter or margarine and fry a chopped onion until soft.
Stir in a little flour and cook briefly.
Gradually add the reserved cooking liquid, stirring until smooth and thickened.
Add the sprouts and carrots back to the sauce, mix and serve.
notes: The same onion sauce can be used with other vegetables such as swede or turnips.

title: Potato Salad I
description: Potato salad with egg, onion and salad cream.
ingredients: 4-6 spring onions, or shallots, or 1 small-medium onion
method: Hard-boil the eggs and set aside in cold water.
Peel or scrub the potatoes and cut into cubes.
Cover with water, bring to the boil and simmer for about 5 minutes until cooked.
Drain and allow to cool.
Chop the onions finely.
Chop the hard-boiled eggs.
Mix the salad cream and milk in a bowl.
Add the onions and chopped egg, then fold in the potato gradually.
notes: Chopped gherkin, pickled cucumber or herbs can also be added.

title: Potato Salad II
description: Warm potato salad with onion and vinaigrette dressing.
method: Cube the potatoes, cover with water, bring to the boil and simmer for about 5 minutes.
Drain and place in a bowl with the onion and pickled cucumber if using.
Pour over the vinaigrette while the potatoes are still hot and mix well.
Allow to cool and refrigerate.

title: Cucumber Salad I
description: Cucumber salad with lemon juice, sugar, dill and salt.
method: Put the cucumber in a bowl and layer with sprinkles of salt.
Leave to stand for a few hours.
Mix the lemon juice with the dill and sugar until the sugar dissolves.
Drain the cucumber well.
Add the cucumber to the lemon dressing and mix.
Refrigerate before serving.
notes: More sweetened lemon juice can be added if needed.

title: Cucumber Salad II
description: Sliced cucumber mixed with cucumber relish and vinaigrette.
method: Mix the cucumber relish and vinaigrette dressing together.
Mix with the sliced cucumber.
Refrigerate before serving.

title: Cucumber Salad III
description: Another simple cucumber salad with relish and vinaigrette.
method: Mix the cucumber relish and vinaigrette dressing together.
Mix with the sliced cucumber.
Refrigerate before serving.

title: Chinese Style Cold Slaw
description: A crunchy cabbage, carrot and spring onion slaw with a sweet-sour dressing.
ingredients: For the dressing: 6 tbsp rice vinegar
For the dressing: 1 tsp ground black pepper
For the dressing: scant 1/3 cup sunflower oil
For the dressing: a pinch of garlic
method: Shred the cabbage and slice the spring onions.
Mix the cabbage, carrots, onions and sesame seeds if using.
Mix the dressing ingredients together thoroughly.
Pour the dressing over the slaw and stir well.
Serve chilled or at cool room temperature.

title: Cabbage Salad
description: A simple shredded salad of cabbage, cucumber, tomato and onion with oil and vinegar.
method: Shred or chop all the ingredients.
Mix together with oil and vinegar.
Serve fresh.

title: Mixed Salad
description: A mixed salad of cabbage, cucumber, tomato, chicory, pepper and cauliflower.
method: Shred or chop all the ingredients.
Mix together.
Add the dressing of your choice before serving.

title: Mrs Griffith’s Salad
description: A chopped salad with cabbage, carrot, onion, peppers, cucumber and celery.
ingredients: 1/4 white cabbage, finely shredded and washed
method: Prepare and combine all the vegetables.
Add salt, pepper, oil, vinegar and mayonnaise to taste.
Mix and serve.

title: Stage Salad
description: A simple chopped salad of cabbage, tomato, cucumber, celery and carrot.
method: Mix all the prepared ingredients together.
Add dressing to taste and serve.

title: Courgette Salad
description: Very finely sliced courgettes mixed with dressing and peanuts.
method: Slice the courgettes very finely.
Mix with dressing and peanuts.
If liked, mix a little peanut butter into the dressing.

title: Piperonata
description: A soft pepper and tomato dish served hot or cold.
ingredients: 6 large red, green and yellow peppers, seeded, cored and cut into thin strips
8 large tomatoes, skinned and coarsely chopped
method: Sweat the onion and peppers together with the butter and oil in a heavy-based saucepan.
Cover and fry until the vegetables are soft but not brown.
Add the remaining ingredients and season to taste.
Continue cooking gently, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are soft and the juices have largely evaporated.
Serve hot or cold, but not chilled.
notes: This is the vegetable-dishes version and kept separate from the Starters version.
